text.title: immersive-design-principles Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for immersive-design-principles. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Pick one immersion level per scene, transition deliberately. Mixed signals (head-locked + world-locked together) cause disorientation.
rule.rationale: Conflicting reference frames break spatial orientation. Clarity on whether UI is fixed to head or world prevents mode confusion and motion sickness.

rule.statement: Comfort vignette during locomotion (snap turn or smooth) is non-negotiable below 90 Hz. Arm-rest poses are required for sessions > 10 min. Fixed reference points prevent motion sickness; clear grounding with stable horizon prevents disorientation.
rule.rationale: Below 90 Hz, visual latency causes vection (perceived self-motion). Vignette (darkening peripheral vision) reduces nausea-inducing optical flow. Arm support and seated-mode reduce fatigue-driven dropout. Horizon stability anchors spatial perception.

rule.statement: Agents may draft immersion-level decisions and comfort checklists. Every motion/locomotion decision MUST be verified in-headset with ≥3 testers for ≥30 minutes before shipping.
rule.rationale: LLMs can plausibly argue for any motion pattern. Vestibular response cannot be predicted from text; only embodied testing reveals nausea-inducing patterns.
